---
title: "ir live demo"
subtitle: "One project, multiple R package timelines"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: simple
    slide-number: true
    code-line-numbers: false
execute:
  eval: false
---

## User pain

A project can contain old production scripts and newer analysis work.

Upgrading the project library to make new tidyverse syntax available can break
old code. Freezing the whole project keeps old code working but blocks newer
syntax.

## The demo setup

```console
examples/live-demo/
  01-legacy-tidyverse.R
  02-modern-tidyverse.R
  03-legacy-tidyverse-report.qmd
  04-modern-tidyverse-report.qmd
```

Each file declares its own dependency timeline.

## Legacy script frontmatter

```r
#| dependencies:
#|   - dplyr==1.0.10
#|   - tidyr==1.2.1
#| exclude-newer: "2022-12-31"
```

The body uses older tidyverse syntax:

```r
orders %>%
  tidyr::separate(account_region, into = c("account", "region"), sep = "\\|") %>%
  dplyr::group_by(account, region) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(tickets = sum(tickets), .groups = "drop")
```

## Run the legacy script

```console
ir run examples/live-demo/01-legacy-tidyverse.R
```

Watch for:

```text
dialect=legacy tidyverse
exclude_newer=2022-12-31
dplyr=...
tidyr=...
library=...
```

## Modern script frontmatter

```r
#| dependencies:
#|   - dplyr==1.1.4
#|   - tidyr==1.3.1
#| exclude-newer: "2024-06-01"
```

The body uses newer tidyverse syntax:

```r
orders |>
  tidyr::separate_wider_delim(account_region, delim = "|", names = c("account", "region")) |>
  dplyr::summarise(tickets = sum(tickets), .by = c(account, region))
```

## Run the modern script

```console
ir run examples/live-demo/02-modern-tidyverse.R
```

Watch for a different dependency timeline:

```text
dialect=modern tidyverse
exclude_newer=2024-06-01
dplyr=...
tidyr=...
library=...
```

## Side by side terminal view

Run these in two panes from the same checkout:

```console
ir run examples/live-demo/01-legacy-tidyverse.R
ir run examples/live-demo/02-modern-tidyverse.R
```

The scripts share the project, but not the resolved dependency library.

## Same idea for Quarto

```console
ir run examples/live-demo/03-legacy-tidyverse-report.qmd
ir run examples/live-demo/04-modern-tidyverse-report.qmd
```

The `ir:` key in each document controls the render-time package timeline:

```yaml
ir:
  dependencies:
    - dplyr==1.1.4
    - tidyr==1.3.1
  exclude-newer: "2024-06-01"
```

## What changed for the user

Before:

```console
Rscript old-script.R
quarto render new-report.qmd
```

Both commands depend on whichever library is active for the project.

After:

```console
ir run old-script.R
ir run new-report.qmd
```

Each file carries the dependency contract it needs.

## Closing sequence

1. Run the legacy script.
2. Run the modern script.
3. Render the legacy Quarto report.
4. Render the modern Quarto report.
5. Re-run one command to show the cache hit path.
